content: Just as the educational definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive step to connect language learning experience to long-term conceptual understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this pedagogical framing to move beyond isolated in-context self-correction and build structured, cumulative 'learning reflection journals' for autonomous agents. This hybrid framework treats each self-reflection step not just as an error-correction tool, but as a vocabulary of past problem-solving experience that accumulates to improve general reasoning over time, similar to how repeated reflexion builds linguistic proficiency in learners.
